intent: To build a secure, user-friendly e-commerce platform that enables seamless product browsing, shopping cart management, and integrated digital payment processing.

entities.name: Web Frontend
entities.description: A mobile-responsive web application that allows users to search and browse products, manage their shopping cart, and initiate the checkout process.

entities.name: Identity Service
entities.description: Secures the platform by managing user registration, authentication, and session states required for checkout and order history tracking.

entities.name: Catalog & Inventory Service
entities.description: Maintains product data, categories, pricing, and real-time inventory levels, serving requests to both customers and administrators.

entities.name: Cart & Order Service
entities.description: Manages active user shopping carts, coordinates the checkout pipeline, and stores completed order records.

entities.name: Payment Gateway Link
entities.description: A secure integration with third-party payment processors like Stripe or PayPal to securely handle credit and debit card transactions.

entities.name: Tax & Shipping API Client
entities.description: A connector that communicates with external APIs to dynamically calculate accurate tax rates and shipping costs during checkout.

entities.name: Admin Dashboard
entities.description: A secure administrative portal enabling staff to manage product listings, track stock levels, and monitor customer orders.

architecture.description: The system flow begins with the user browsing products on the Web Frontend, which queries the Catalog & Inventory Service. Users can add items to their cart, managed by the Cart & Order Service. To checkout, the user must authenticate via the Identity Service. Once authenticated, the Cart & Order Service calculates taxes and shipping fees via the Tax & Shipping API Client, then redirects the payment process to the secure Payment Gateway Link. Upon payment confirmation, the order is finalized, inventory is updated, and the Admin Dashboard is updated to allow administrators to track and fulfill the new order.

endpoints.summary: Register a new user account with the platform.
validation_rules: email must be unique and valid format
password must be at least 8 characters long

endpoints.summary: Authenticate user and return session JWT token.
validation_rules: email and password are required fields

endpoints.summary: Search and browse products with real-time stock information.
Create a new product listing (Admin only).
validation_rules: price must be a positive number
stock_quantity must be a non-negative integer

endpoints.summary: Retrieve the current active shopping cart items.
Add or update an item in the shopping cart.
validation_rules: product_id must exist in products database
quantity must be greater than zero

endpoints.summary: Initiate checkout, calculate tax/shipping, and create an order.
validation_rules: shipping_address is required and must contain valid geographic codes for tax/shipping calculation

endpoints.summary: Handle webhook payment notifications from Stripe/PayPal and capture payment status.
validation_rules: amount must match order total amount

endpoints.summary: List customer orders for administrative tracking and fulfillment.
Fulfill an order and create shipment tracking details.
validation_rules: order_id must reference a valid paid order

rules.logic: Before redirecting to the Payment Gateway Link, the Cart & Order Service must query the Catalog & Inventory Service to verify that the stock levels for all items in the cart are greater than or equal to the requested quantities. If stock is insufficient, the checkout process is halted and the user is prompted to adjust their cart.

To prevent anonymous transactions, the Cart & Order Service must validate that the active session token is authentic and active via the Identity Service before calculating tax/shipping or accepting payment details.

The Cart & Order Service must dynamically retrieve accurate tax and shipping costs from the Tax & Shipping API Client using the user's validated shipping address before finalizing the order invoice and passing the transaction total to the Payment Gateway Link.

Upon receiving a successful payment confirmation from the Payment Gateway Link, the Cart & Order Service must update the order state to 'Paid', notify the Catalog & Inventory Service to permanently decrement the purchased item quantities, and trigger an alert on the Admin Dashboard.

The Admin Dashboard must receive real-time state updates whenever an order transitions to 'Paid' or 'Cancelled' status to prevent staff from fulfilling unpaid or aborted orders and to ensure accurate logistics tracking.
